behaviors.description: Trim conversation history to the most recent 10 messages before LLM call when history is too long.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Yes. A unit test of a trim helper can pass while prompt-construction wiring still sends the wrong slice/order to the LLM client. REAL-INFRA: No OS/network requirement to catch this. MOCK-HIDE: Mocking only the trim function can hide failures in module composition. LLM-DEP: No. MULTI-STEP: No.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real request-handling stack in-process with a local fake LLM transport that records payloads. Submit 15-message history, execute chatbot handler, and assert the outbound LLM request contains exactly the latest 10 messages in correct chronological order and role metadata.

behaviors.description: Generated response is on-topic and answers the customer’s actual question.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Yes. Deterministic mocks cannot expose semantic irrelevance from a real model. REAL-INFRA: Real model/API behavior is the failure boundary, not just local wiring. MOCK-HIDE: Yes, mocked LLM outputs will trivially look correct. LLM-DEP: Yes, correctness depends on real LLM content and can vary run-to-run. MULTI-STEP: Not required if evaluating single-generation quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use real production-like prompts and call the real LLM with a fixed set of customer questions plus rubric-based grading (human-labeled or model-judge with strict acceptance thresholds). Fail if relevance/answer-rate drops below threshold across repeated runs.

behaviors.description: Tone re-check flags an overly formal response for casual-tier customers and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Yes. Mocking tone outcomes can miss control-flow bugs between initial generation, tone check, and retry. REAL-INFRA: External OS/network not the key boundary; agent pipeline behavior is. MOCK-HIDE: Yes, forced mock flags can always pass without validating real tone detection behavior. LLM-DEP: Yes, tone judgment and regenerated content depend on real LLM behavior. MULTI-STEP: Yes, requires sequential agentic steps (generate -> evaluate tone -> regenerate).
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end chatbot pipeline with real LLM calls on prompts designed to elicit formal wording for casual-tier users. Verify trace/logs show first draft generated, tone-check rejection event, and second generation attempt; assert final accepted response differs from rejected draft and meets casual-tier tone criteria.

behaviors.description: Persist full exchange (user message, first draft, regenerations, timestamps) correctly in DB.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Yes. Repository/unit mocks can miss schema/transaction/timestamp mapping bugs. REAL-INFRA: Local DB integration is sufficient; no need for external system. MOCK-HIDE: Yes, mocked persistence often always succeeds and hides serialization/order defects. LLM-DEP: No, can inject deterministic response artifacts. MULTI-STEP: Agentic sequence not required to validate persistence shape.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler with local test DB (real schema/migrations). Force a regeneration path using deterministic stubs for model outputs, then assert stored records include user input, initial draft, regenerated draft, linkage/correlation IDs, and timestamps with correct ordering and timezone handling.

behaviors.description: If tone-triggered regeneration occurs, only the second response is returned to customer; rejected draft is never exposed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Yes. Unit tests on selector logic can miss leaks in controller/serializer/event wiring. REAL-INFRA: Usually no external OS/network needed; in-process API layer is enough unless streaming transport is involved. MOCK-HIDE: Yes, mocking downstream output channel can hide transient first-draft emission bugs. LLM-DEP: No, can force trigger with deterministic fixtures. MULTI-STEP: Not inherently agentic for this assertion; it is output-selection correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the real API/application stack in-process with forced tone-reject then regenerate path. Capture the customer-facing response payload/stream and assert only regenerated text appears, while internal logs/DB retain rejected draft marked non-customer-visible.
